autonomous_research_brief.name: Autonomous Research Brief
autonomous_research_brief.description: Performs deep research on a topic and produces a synthesized brief.

steps.description: Search the web for relevant information on the topic.
Summarize the search results into key takeaways.
Format the summary into a professional research brief.

executive_daily_briefing.description: Aggregates calendar and emails into a concise morning briefing.

strategic_opportunity_monitor.name: Strategic Opportunity Monitor
strategic_opportunity_monitor.description: Analyzes external events for strategic alignment and notifies the executive.

steps.description: Analyze the incoming external event payload.
Check the event analysis against the user's strategic goals.
Notify the executive of the high-alignment opportunity.

results.title: Quantum Computing in 2026: From Laboratory Curiosity to Practical ...

But 2026 is proving to be the year when the technology begins to transition from laboratory curiosity to practical, if narrow, utility. results.title: [2511.09030] Solving a Million-Step LLM Task with Zero Errors
results.snippet: This paper describes MAKER, the first system that successfully solves a task with over one million LLM steps with zero errors, and, in principle, scales far beyond this level. The approach relies on an extreme decomposition of a task into subtasks, each of which can be tackled by focused microagents.

results.title: Shattering the Illusion: MAKER Achieves Million-Step, Zero-Error LLM ...
results.snippet: Figure 2. Orthogonal directions to scaling AI MAKER takes an orthogonal path, scaling reliability instead of model size. The chart compares leading LLMs by their error-free step length versus cost, showing that while single models fail after hundreds of steps, MAKER completes over one million with zero errors. A million-step evaluation: Towers of Hanoi, zero errors To test the theory, the team ...

results.title: Solving a Million-Step LLM Task with Zero Errors
results.snippet: This paper describes MAKER, the first system that successfully solves a task with over one million LLM steps with zero errors, and, in principle, scales far beyond this level.

results.title: Paper page - Solving a Million-Step LLM Task with Zero Errors
results.snippet: Join the discussion on this paper page This paper shows how even relatively small non-reasoning LLMs can be scaled to completing tasks with over a million steps with zero errors, and in theory can scale far beyond that. It relies on being able to decompose a task into small enough subtasks so that sampling a correct answer is somewhat likely, and no incorrect answer is more likely. How to ...

results.title: Solving a Million-Step LLM Task with Zero Errors: The Power of ...
results.snippet: A groundbreaking paper introduces MAKER, a system that solves a million-step LLM task with zero errors by breaking problems into tiny pieces and voting on each step. It's a new path to scalable, reliable AI.
